questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: Which of the following legal provisions is correctly matched with its specific function in the regulation of online gaming and betting?

options.text: Section 69A of IT Act, 2000 — Registration of Intermediaries with Self-regulatory bodies
Section 112 of Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 — Penalties for unauthorised betting and gambling
Consumer Protection Act, 2019 — Blocking access to illegal betting websites and mobile apps
IT Rules, 2021 — Prohibition of misleading advertisements related to gaming

questions.explanation: Section 112 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), 2023, specifically penalises unauthorised betting and gambling. Section 69A of the IT Act is used for blocking illegal websites. The IT Rules 2021 provide norms for registration with self-regulatory bodies. The Consumer Protection Act 2019 prohibits misleading advertisements.
questions.takeaway: Multiple legal frameworks (IT Act, BNS, and Consumer Protection Act) converge to regulate different aspects of the online gaming ecosystem.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Section 69A of IT Act, 2000: Empowers Government to block access to illegal websites... IT Rules, 2021: Provide norms for online gaming platforms. E.g. Registration of Intermediaries with Self-regulatory bodies... Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023: Section 111 and 112 penalise unlawful economic activities and cybercrimes and unauthorised betting and gambling respectively... Consumer Protection Act, 2019: Prohibits misleading advertisements.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: The National Sports Policy (NSP) 2025 is structured around five core pillars. Which of the following is NOT explicitly mentioned as a focus area of these pillars?

options.text: Integrating sports with the formal education system
Promoting economic and social development through sports
Establishing a mandatory quota for athletes in legislative bodies
Transforming sports into a people's movement

questions.explanation: The NSP 2025 pillars focus on global excellence, economic and social development, making sports a people movement, and integrating sports with education. There is no mention of mandatory quotas for athletes in legislative bodies.
questions.takeaway: The NSP 2025 aims for holistic development and integration of sports into the social and educational fabric of India.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: It is structured around five core pillars, focusing on global excellence, economic and social development through sports, making sports a people movement, and integrating sports with education.
